mission: Make urban mobility easier to operate, pay for, and optimize by connecting parking, transport, fleets, partners, and city intelligence on one scalable global platform.

businessModel: Partner ecosystem take rate and attach revenue
marketForces: Cities want digital curb and parking control without operational disruption
Transport authorities are modernizing toward open payment and better operational visibility
Global mobility platforms need reusable partner connectivity instead of market-by-market integration
Private-equity-backed consolidation increases pressure to turn many brands into one operating platform
Automotive, fleet, and city-planning use cases are converging around data, payment, and policy infrastructure

designPrinciples: Organize around value streams where customer economics and operating workflows are meaningfully different: parking, transit, business mobility, ecosystem growth, and city intelligence.
Centralize the platform layers every business line needs: identity, integration, ledger, workflow, data, and reliability.
Treat payments, settlement, and partner governance as product capabilities with explicit ownership rather than shared back-office afterthoughts.
Keep local market delivery and commercial packaging flexible while capability ownership remains global.
Use the rebrand to converge product and data operating models before forcing all market-facing brands into one surface.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own parking operator workflows, curb configuration, compliance, and asset monetization.
Modernize fare collection, device operations, and revenue assurance for transport operators.
Own business-account policy, billing, and employee or driver parking experiences.
Scale ecosystem integrations, market onboarding, and in-car partner services.
teams: Partner success and commercial enablement team

recommendedStructure.mission: Turn operational mobility data into policy-grade insight and planning workflows.
Provide the shared foundations required for integration, scale, governance, and cross-portfolio leverage.
Guide portfolio convergence, architecture, governance, and M&A integration.

operatingModelNotes: The operating challenge is not lack of product scope but turning acquired assets into a coherent platform and data model.
Product lines should retain local brand flexibility while shared capabilities converge in architecture, data, and governance.
A 500-person company can support this breadth only if partner, payment, workflow, and data foundations are reused rather than rebuilt per business line.
